communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA US Navy 4/20 Vietnam War
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Military service records for subject below;
Name: George Bovine
Branch: United States Navy
DOB: Feb 1, 1948
Dates of Service: Approx 1967 to 1975

Request following documents:
1) Bronze Star Medal award citations
2) History of Assignments NAVPERS form 601-5
3) cumulative Navy Occupational training history NAVPERS 601-4 and 1070/604,
4) military education and courses
5) Navy commendation medal award citation

Historical Documentation, Vietnam War history

first name: George
Middle initial: L
last name: Bovine

I request hard copies of paperwork in the file showing this information.
Please mail hardcopies to:

MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON, MA 02115
or please email pdf copies to requests@muckrock.com

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

title: Preciado Capital Murder Trial Exhibits - Records Request (290th District Court, 2026)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request copies of the following records related to the capital murder trial of Christopher Preciado (defendant), which concluded with a guilty verdict on March 26, 2026, in the 290th Judicial District Court of Bexar County, Texas. The case involved the December 2023 deaths of Savanah Soto and Matthew Guerra.

Because the trial is concluded and a verdict has been rendered, the following records are no longer part of an active investigation and are subject to disclosure:

1. The complete exhibit list / evidence log admitted at trial
2. All video exhibits admitted into evidence, including but not limited to:
   a. Any custodial interview / interrogation video of Christopher Preciado
   b. Any Ring / residential / business surveillance video admitted as exhibits (including Danny Kaye Drive area footage)
   c. Any police body-worn camera video admitted as exhibits
3. All audio exhibits admitted into evidence, including any 911 call recordings
4. All photographic exhibits (crime scene photos, evidence photos, diagrams) admitted at trial
5. All documentary exhibits admitted at trial (phone records, digital forensics reports, autopsy photos where not sealed, etc.)
6. The indictment and the final judgment/sentencing order
7. The docket sheet for the case

If a cause number is needed to locate the file, please search by defendant name "Christopher Preciado" in the 290th District Court; I was unable to retrieve the cause number through the public Justice Information Portal. I am happy to provide any additional identifying information on request.

Please provide records in electronic format (email attachment, download link, or CD/USB) where possible to minimize cost. I am willing to pay reasonable duplication fees up to $100 without prior approval; please notify me if fees will exceed that amount before producing records.

In the event that any portion of this request is denied or any records are sealed, please cite the specific statute or court order supporting withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response within the timeframe required by the Texas Public Information Act (Gov. Code Ch. 552).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ka Yi Lo
